units.title: Debates Estructurados
units.description: Presenta, defiende, concede y refuta con estructura de debate avanzado en ingles.

reading.text_en:
Advanced debate in English has a clear architecture:

Opening position: The evidence overwhelmingly supports the view that...
Developing the argument: Not only does this reduce costs, but it also improves quality.
Anticipating opposition: One might argue that... however, this overlooks...
Conceding strategically: I concede that this approach carries risk. Nevertheless...
Powerful close: On balance, the case for X is compelling — the evidence is clear, the precedent exists, and the risk is manageable.
reading.text_es: El debate avanzado tiene una arquitectura clara. Posicion de apertura: La evidencia apoya abrumadoramente... Desarrollar: No solo reduce costos, sino que tambien mejora la calidad. Anticipar oposicion: Algunos podrian argumentar... sin embargo, esto pasa por alto... Concesion estrategica: Concedo que este enfoque conlleva riesgo. Sin embargo...
vocab: one might argue that... however, this overlooks...
en balance / tomando todo en cuenta
the case for X is compelling
el argumento para X es convincente

grammar.tip_text: La concesion estrategica es la herramienta mas poderosa del debatiente avanzado. Al admitir debilidades controladas de tu argumento, ganas credibilidad. Formula: I concede that [weakness]. Nevertheless/However, [stronger counter]. Esto demuestra pensamiento critico y madurez intelectual.
table: Not only is it cheaper, but it is faster.
One might argue... however, this overlooks...
One might claim... but this ignores the fact that...
I concede the risk. Nevertheless, the benefits outweigh it.

dialogue.scene: Debate estructurado sobre un tema complejo
lines: A
Should companies be legally required to report their carbon emissions?
Las empresas deberian estar legalmente obligadas a reportar sus emisiones de carbono?
B
The evidence overwhelmingly supports mandatory disclosure. Not only does transparency drive accountability, but it also enables investors to make informed decisions.
La evidencia apoya abrumadoramente la divulgacion obligatoria. No solo la transparencia impulsa la responsabilidad, sino que tambien permite a los inversores tomar decisiones informadas.
A
But that places a disproportionate burden on smaller businesses.
Pero eso coloca una carga desproporcionada en las empresas mas pequenas.
B
One might argue that. However, this overlooks the tiered framework most proposals include — exemptions exist for SMEs below a certain threshold.
Algunos podrian argumentar eso. Sin embargo, esto pasa por alto el marco escalonado que la mayoria de las propuestas incluyen: existen exenciones para las PYMES por debajo de cierto umbral.
A
I concede that. Nevertheless, compliance costs remain a concern.
Lo concedo. Sin embargo, los costos de cumplimiento siguen siendo una preocupacion.
B
On balance, the case for mandatory reporting is compelling. The long-term systemic benefits — investor confidence, regulatory clarity, competitive fairness — outweigh the short-term friction.

En balance, el argumento para el reporte obligatorio es convincente. Los beneficios sistemicos a largo plazo: confianza de inversores, claridad regulatoria, equidad competitiva, superan la friccion a corto plazo.

speaking.scene: Debate estructurado sobre un tema complejo
speaking.prompt: "Take a position on a controversial topic and defend it through structured debate."
speaking.response_hint: "The evidence overwhelmingly supports... / Not only... but also... / One might argue... however, this overlooks... / I concede... nevertheless... / On balance..."
phrases: One might argue that... however, this overlooks...
On balance, the case is compelling.
The argument for X outweighs the case against.

chips: One might argue that... however, this overlooks...
On balance, the case is compelling.
The argument for X outweighs the case against.

units.title: Explicar Temas Tecnicos Simples
units.description: Comunicar conceptos tecnicos o especializados a audiencias no tecnicas. La habilidad del experto completo.

reading.text_en:
The ability to explain technical topics to non-technical audiences is one of the most valued skills in professional English:

Simplify without dumbing down: Essentially, blockchain is a shared ledger that cannot be altered retroactively.
Analogies: Think of it like a Google Doc that millions of people share simultaneously — but no one can delete past entries.
Layer complexity: At a basic level... / Going deeper... / For those with a technical background...
Check comprehension: Does that give you a clear picture? / Shall I go into more detail?
reading.text_es: Explicar temas tecnicos a audiencias no tecnicas es una de las habilidades mas valoradas. Simplificar sin trivializar: Esencialmente, blockchain es un libro mayor compartido que no puede ser alterado retroactivamente. Analogias: Piensalo como un Google Doc que millones de personas comparten. Capas de complejidad: En un nivel basico... / Profundizando...
vocab: think of it like / imagine it as...
at a basic level / going deeper...
does that give you a clear picture?
in technical terms / in layperson's terms
the key takeaway for a non-technical audience is...
extra_vocab: does that give you a clear picture

grammar.tip_text: Explica en 3 capas: CAPA 1 (nivel junior): analogia simple, sin jargon. CAPA 2 (nivel medio): estructura basica, terminologia clave. CAPA 3 (nivel experto): detalles tecnicos, matices, limitaciones. Pregunta primero: What is your background on this? — asi sabes desde que capa empezar.
table: At its core, AI is pattern recognition.
Think of it like a very fast calculator.
Going deeper, the algorithm learns iteratively.
Does that give you a clear picture?
Shall I go into more detail?

dialogue.scene: Explicando un concepto tecnico a alguien sin contexto
lines: A
Can you explain what machine learning actually does — in plain terms?
Puedes explicar que hace realmente el machine learning, en terminos sencillos?
B

At its core, machine learning is about teaching computers to learn from examples rather than explicit rules. Think of it like this: instead of programming a computer to detect spam, you show it a million emails labeled spam or not spam — and it figures out the patterns.

En su nucleo, el machine learning consiste en ensenar a las computadoras a aprender de ejemplos en vez de reglas explicitas. Piensalo asi: en vez de programar una computadora para detectar spam, le muestras un millon de emails etiquetados como spam o no spam, y ella descubre los patrones.
A
And how does that differ from traditional programming?
Y en que se diferencia de la programacion tradicional?
B

Going deeper — in traditional programming, humans write every rule. In machine learning, the algorithm writes the rules itself based on data. The key takeaway for a non-technical audience: it gets smarter the more data it sees. Does that give you a clear picture?

Profundizando: en la programacion tradicional, los humanos escriben cada regla. En machine learning, el algoritmo escribe las reglas el mismo basandose en datos. El punto clave para una audiencia no tecnica: se vuelve mas inteligente cuantos mas datos ve. Te da una imagen clara?
A
Yes, very clear. Can you give a real-world business example?
Si, muy claro. Puedes dar un ejemplo de negocio del mundo real?
B
Of course. When Netflix recommends a show, that is machine learning — it has learned your preferences from thousands of data points and predicts what you will enjoy next.
Por supuesto. Cuando Netflix recomienda una serie, eso es machine learning: ha aprendido tus preferencias a partir de miles de puntos de datos y predice lo que disfrutaras a continuacion.

speaking.prompt: "Explain a technical concept from your field to someone who has no background."
speaking.response_hint: "At its core... / Think of it like... / Going deeper... / In layperson's terms... / Does that give you a clear picture?"
phrases: Going deeper, the technical reality is...
Does that give you a clear picture?

Does that give you a clear picture?

units.title: Expresar Opiniones Profundas
units.description: Del opinion basica a la opinion fundamentada con matiz, evidencia y perspectiva critica.

reading.text_en:
At B2 level, opinions are not just preferences — they are argued positions with nuance, evidence, and intellectual honesty:

I tend to think that... although I recognize the counterarguments.
My view is nuanced — on one hand..., but on the other hand...
The evidence points toward..., though I would caveat that...
It is a complex issue, but if I had to take a position, I would argue that...
I have changed my view on this — I used to think..., but now I believe...
reading.text_es: En B2, las opiniones son posiciones argumentadas con matiz, evidencia y honestidad intelectual. Tiendo a pensar que... aunque reconozco los contraargumentos. Mi perspectiva es matizada: por un lado..., pero por el otro... La evidencia apunta hacia..., aunque advertiria que...
vocab: I have changed my view on this
if I had to take a position, I would argue...
extra_vocab: if I had to take a position
si tuviera que tomar una posicion

grammar.tip_text: Los pensadores mas sofisticados no tienen respuestas absolutas — tienen posiciones informadas y abiertas a revision. Frases que demuestran esto: I tend to think... (no absolutista). I would caveat that... (auto-critico). I have changed my view... (evolucion intelectual). That said... (equilibrio). These are intellectually honest phrases that native advanced speakers use constantly.
table: I tend to think regulation will increase.
My view is nuanced — on one hand...
On one hand efficiency, on the other, quality.
I would caveat that the data has limitations.
I have changed my view on this.
I used to think X, but now I believe Y.

dialogue.scene: Expresando una opinion compleja y matizada
lines: A
What is your view on the future of work? Will offices become obsolete?
Cual es tu opinion sobre el futuro del trabajo? Las oficinas se volvaran obsoletas?
B
My view is nuanced. On one hand, the evidence clearly points toward a permanent shift to hybrid work — productivity data, retention data, and talent pool data all support this.
Mi perspectiva es matizada. Por un lado, la evidencia apunta claramente hacia un cambio permanente hacia el trabajo hibrido: datos de productividad, retencion y talento lo respaldan.
A
And on the other hand?
Y por el otro?
B

On the other hand, I would caveat that the social and creative benefits of in-person work are genuinely undervalued in those studies. Collaboration, culture, mentorship — these are harder to quantify.

Por el otro lado, advertiria que los beneficios sociales y creativos del trabajo presencial estan genuinamente subestimados en esos estudios. Colaboracion, cultura, "mentoria": son mas dificiles de cuantificar.
A
Has your view on this changed?
Ha cambiado tu perspectiva sobre esto?
B
It has, actually. I used to think offices would disappear entirely. Now I believe we are moving toward purpose-driven office use — people come in for collaboration, not execution.

Si, en realidad. Antes pensaba que las oficinas desaparecerian por completo. Ahora creo que nos movemos hacia un uso de oficinas orientado a proposito: la gente viene para colaborar, no para ejecutar.

speaking.scene: Expresando una opinion compleja y matizada
speaking.prompt: "Give your opinion on a complex topic. Include nuance, evidence, and intellectual honesty."
speaking.response_hint: "I tend to think... / My view is nuanced — on one hand... / I would caveat that... / If I had to take a position... / I have changed my view on this."
phrases: My view is nuanced — on one hand...
I have changed my view on this.

chips: My view is nuanced — on one hand...
I have changed my view on this.

units.title: Interaccion en Reuniones en Ingles
units.description: De participante a contributor activo. El lenguaje para intervenir, facilitar y liderar reuniones en ingles.

reading.text_en:
Meetings in English require a specific set of skills — not just language, but meeting management strategies:

Starting: Let us get started. The purpose of today's session is to...
Taking the floor: If I could just come in here... / I would like to build on that point...
Checking understanding: Could you elaborate on that? / Just to clarify — are you suggesting...?
Moving forward: Shall we move on? / Let us park that discussion and return to the main agenda.
Action items: To summarize the actions: X will... by Friday. Y is responsible for...
reading.text_es: Las reuniones en ingles requieren habilidades especificas. Iniciar: Empecemos. El objetivo de hoy es... Tomar la palabra: Si me permiten intervenir... / Me gustaria desarrollar ese punto. Verificar comprension: Podria elaborar sobre eso? Avanzar: Seguimos adelante? / Estacionemos esa discusion. Acciones: Para resumir las acciones: X hara... para el viernes.
vocab: let us get started / the purpose today is
if I could just come in here...
just to clarify — are you suggesting...?
let us park that and return to...
estacionar un tema fuera de agenda
extra_vocab: if I could just come in

grammar.tip_text: En reuniones en ingles, interrumpir educadamente es una habilidad clave. Frases: If I could just come in here... / Sorry to interrupt, but I wanted to add... / Building on that point... / Can I just pick up on something you said? Nunca interrumpas abruptamente. El ingles profesional valora la cortesia estrategica sobre la agresividad.
table: Let us get started. The purpose today is...
Good morning. Let us begin — today's goal is...
If I could just come in here...
Sorry to interrupt — if I could add...
Could you say a bit more about that?
Let us park that and return to...
Let us table that and come back to it.

dialogue.scene: Facilitando y participando en una reunion
lines: F
Good morning everyone. Let us get started — we have a tight agenda. The purpose of today's session is to align on the Q4 roadmap.
Buenos dias a todos. Empecemos: tenemos una agenda ajustada. El objetivo de hoy es alinearnos en el roadmap del Q4.
A
Before we dive in — if I could just come in here — I wanted to flag that the engineering team has a dependency that affects our timeline.
Antes de comenzar: si me permiten intervenir, queria señalar que el equipo de ingenieria tiene una dependencia que afecta nuestro cronograma.
F
Thank you for raising that. Could you elaborate on the dependency briefly?
Gracias por mencionarlo. Podrias elaborar brevemente sobre la dependencia?
A
Sure. We are waiting on API access from the third party — without it, we cannot start the integration sprint.
Claro. Estamos esperando acceso a la API de un tercero: sin eso, no podemos iniciar el sprint de integracion.
F
Understood. Let us park that for now and add it as a standing item — it needs its own discussion. Just to clarify — is the Q4 date still achievable pending that?
Entendido. Estacionemos eso por ahora y añadamoslo como punto recurrente: necesita su propia discusion. Solo para clarificar: la fecha del Q4 sigue siendo alcanzable pendiendo de eso?
A
Yes, if the access comes through by end of next week.
Si, si el acceso llega para el final de la proxima semana.
F
Perfect. To summarize the actions: our tech lead will follow up with the API vendor by Thursday. Maria will present the revised timeline by Friday. Any objections?
Perfecto. Para resumir las acciones: nuestro tech lead hara seguimiento con el proveedor de la API para el jueves. Maria presentara el cronograma revisado para el viernes. Alguna objecion?

speaking.scene: Facilitando y participando en una reunion
speaking.prompt: "Simulate a meeting. One person facilitates, one participates."
speaking.response_hint: "Let us get started. / If I could just come in here... / Could you elaborate? / Let us park that. / To summarize the actions..."
phrases: Let us get started. The purpose today is...
If I could just come in here...
Just to clarify — are you suggesting...?
Let us park that and return to it.

chips: Let us get started. The purpose today is...
If I could just come in here...
Just to clarify — are you suggesting...?
Let us park that and return to it.

units.title: Second and Third Conditional
units.description: El segundo y tercer condicional para situaciones hipoteticas y arrepentimientos. La gramatica que suena profesional.

reading.text_en:
The Second Conditional is for imaginary present/future situations: If I were the CEO, I would restructure the team.

The Third Conditional is for past regrets — things that did not happen: If we had invested earlier, we would have doubled our revenue.

Together they unlock hypothetical thinking, a core skill in professional English: negotiation, analysis, strategy, and critical conversations.
reading.text_es: El Segundo Condicional es para situaciones imaginarias en el presente/futuro: Si yo fuera el CEO, reestructuraria el equipo. El Tercer Condicional es para arrepentimientos del pasado: Si hubieramos invertido antes, habriamos duplicado nuestros ingresos.
vocab: If + past perfect + would have + PP
extra_vocab: Si + past perfect → would have + PP

grammar.tip_text: En el Segundo Condicional formal, siempre usamos WERE (no was) con todos los sujetos: If I WERE you... If she WERE here... En conversacion informal se acepta was, pero en entornos profesionales o escritura formal, WERE es obligatorio.
table: If I were CEO, I would...
If she were here, she would agree.
If we had invested, we would have...
If he had studied, he would have passed.
I wish I had taken that opportunity.
If only we could start over.

lines: A
If you could change one thing about our strategy, what would it be?
Si pudieras cambiar algo de nuestra estrategia, que seria?
B
If I were in charge, I would focus entirely on digital channels. The ROI is undeniable.
Si yo estuviera a cargo, me enfocaria completamente en canales digitales. El ROI es innegable.
A
Good point. If we had done that two years ago, where would we be now?
Buen punto. Si lo hubieramos hecho hace dos anos, donde estariamos ahora?
B
If we had pivoted in 2022, we would have captured the market before our competitors did.
Si hubieramos pivoteado en 2022, habriamos capturado el mercado antes que nuestros competidores.
A
Exactly. If we had listened to the data, we would not have lost that contract.
Exactamente. Si hubieramos escuchado los datos, no habriamos perdido ese contrato.
B
Well, if we implement these changes now, we might still recover significant market share.
Bueno, si implementamos estos cambios ahora, podriamos recuperar una participacion significativa de mercado.

speaking.prompt: "If you could change one decision, what would it be? What would have happened?"
speaking.response_hint: "If I were... / If we had... / I would have... / If only we had..."
phrases: If I were you, I would...
If we had done this, we would have...
What would you have done if...?

chips: If I were you, I would...
If we had done this, we would have...
What would you have done if...?

units.title: Reported Speech
units.description: Reporte lo que dijeron, pidieron y prometieron otros. Esencial para reuniones, emails y actualizaciones.

reading.text_en:
When we report what someone said, we shift the tenses back in time:

Direct: "We will launch next quarter."
Reported: The manager said they would launch the following quarter.

Direct: "Have you reviewed the report?"
Reported: She asked whether I had reviewed the report.

In professional settings, reported speech is critical for: summarizing meetings, writing follow-up emails, and giving updates to stakeholders.
reading.text_es: Cuando reportamos lo que alguien dijo, retrocedemos los tiempos: Directo: Lanzaremos el proximo trimestre. Reportado: El gerente dijo que lanzarian el trimestre siguiente. En entornos profesionales, el reported speech es clave para resumir reuniones y dar actualizaciones.
vocab: el tiempo verbal retrocede un paso
said, told, asked, explained, confirmed, promised

grammar.tip_text: PRESENTE → PASADO: am/is → was. PASADO → PAST PERFECT: went → had gone. WILL → WOULD: will → would. CAN → COULD: can → could. NOW → THEN: today → that day, tomorrow → the next day. Esto aplica cuando el reporte es posterior al momento original.
table: She asked if we had finished.
He confirmed that it would work.

lines: A
What did the client say in the meeting?
Que dijo el cliente en la reunion?
B
She said they were very interested, but that they needed to review the pricing.
Dijo que estaban muy interesados, pero que necesitaban revisar los precios.
A
Did she mention the timeline?
Menciono el plazo?
B
Yes, she asked whether we could deliver by Q3. She also asked how we planned to handle support.
Si, pregunto si podriamos entregar para el Q3. Tambien pregunto como pensabamos manejar el soporte.
A
And what did you tell her?
Y que le dijiste?
B
I told her we would confirm the timeline by Friday and that our team was fully committed.
Le dije que confirmariamos el plazo para el viernes y que nuestro equipo estaba completamente comprometido.

speaking.prompt: "Give me an update. What did they say in the meeting?"
speaking.response_hint: "She said that... / He asked whether... / They told us to... / I confirmed that..."

units.title: Relative Clauses
units.description: That, which, who, where, whose. Combina oraciones y suenas mas fluido e inteligente.

reading.text_en:
Relative clauses add information without starting a new sentence — they make your English sound sophisticated and connected.

Defining: The report that we submitted last week was approved.
Non-defining: The CFO, who joined last month, has already restructured the budget.
Where/When: The office where I work is in the financial district.

The difference between that and which marks you as an advanced speaker.
reading.text_es: Las clausulas relativas agregan informacion sin iniciar una nueva oracion. Definitorias: The report THAT we submitted was approved. No definitorias: The CFO, WHO joined last month, has already restructured the budget. La diferencia entre THAT y WHICH te marca como hablante avanzado.

grammar.tip_text: THAT: clausulas definitorias (sin coma). Da informacion esencial: The proposal THAT we rejected was too expensive. WHICH: clausulas no definitorias (con coma). Agrega informacion extra: The proposal, WHICH was 50 pages long, was rejected. Regla: quita la clausula — si la oracion pierde sentido, usa THAT. Si solo pierdes detalle, usa WHICH.
table: The person that called was my manager.
My boss, who is from London,...
The office where we met was modern.
The moment when she arrived was perfect.

lines: A
Did you speak to the analyst who prepared the Q3 report?
Hablaste con el analista que preparo el informe del Q3?
B
Yes, her name is Diana. She is the one whose analysis changed our entire forecast.
Si, se llama Diana. Ella es la que cuyo analisis cambio toda nuestra proyeccion.
A
What about the software that we discussed last week?
Y el software del que hablamos la semana pasada?
B
The platform, which has been tested by three enterprise clients, is ready for full deployment.
La plataforma, que ha sido probada por tres clientes empresariales, esta lista para implementacion completa.
A
Can you share the document where all the test results are compiled?
Puedes compartir el documento donde estan compilados todos los resultados de prueba?
B
Of course. I will also include the timeline, which shows the full rollout plan.
Por supuesto. Tambien incluire el cronograma, que muestra el plan completo de lanzamiento.

speaking.prompt: "Describe a project, person, or tool using relative clauses."
speaking.response_hint: "The person who... / The system that... / The report, which... / The place where..."

units.title: Modals Avanzados: might have / should have
units.description: Los modales del pasado hipotetico. Para analizar, criticar con tacto y expresar certeza sobre el pasado.

reading.text_en:
Advanced modals let you talk about past possibility, obligation, and deduction — essential for professional analysis.

SHOULD HAVE: past obligation not fulfilled → We should have consulted legal before signing.
COULD HAVE: past possibility not taken → We could have avoided this problem.
MIGHT HAVE: past speculation → The delay might have affected client trust.
MUST HAVE: past deduction → She must have worked all weekend on this.
reading.text_es: Los modales avanzados hablan de posibilidad, obligacion y deduccion en el pasado. SHOULD HAVE: obligacion no cumplida. COULD HAVE: posibilidad no tomada. MIGHT HAVE: especulacion pasada. MUST HAVE: deduccion del pasado. Esenciales para el analisis profesional.

grammar.tip_text: En el trabajo, SHOULD HAVE puede sonar critico. Suavizalo con: Perhaps we should have... / In hindsight, we might have... / Looking back, we could have... Esto hace tu feedback sonar reflexivo, no acusatorio. Clave para entornos profesionales.
table: We should have checked the data.
The error might have caused delays.
She must have worked all night.

lines: A
The project went over budget again. What happened?
El proyecto se paso del presupuesto de nuevo. Que paso?
B
We should have flagged the risk earlier. The warning signs were there in Week 2.
Deberiamos haber senalado el riesgo antes. Las senales de advertencia estaban en la semana 2.
A
Could we have prevented it?
Podriamos haberlo evitado?
B
Absolutely. We could have restructured the scope in Q1. Someone must have known the timeline was unrealistic.
Absolutamente. Podriamos haber reestructurado el alcance en el Q1. Alguien debe haber sabido que el plazo era irrealista.
A
What might have caused the initial underestimate?
Que podria haber causado el subestimate inicial?
B
It might have been overconfidence. We should not have committed without a proper risk assessment.
Podria haber sido exceso de confianza. No deberiamos haber comprometido sin una evaluacion de riesgos adecuada.

speaking.prompt: "Look back at a project or decision. What should have been done differently?"
speaking.response_hint: "We should have... / We could have... / Someone must have... / It might have been..."

units.title: Voz Pasiva Avanzada
units.description: La voz pasiva en todos sus tiempos y contextos profesionales. Suena objetivo, formal y experto.

reading.text_en:
Advanced passive voice extends across all tenses and structures:

Continuous: The system is being upgraded.
Perfect: The contract has been signed.
Modal: This issue should be escalated immediately.
Passive infinitive: The report needs to be submitted by Friday.

In professional writing and presentations, passive voice signals objectivity — you focus on what happened, not who did it.
reading.text_es: La voz pasiva avanzada abarca todos los tiempos: Continua: El sistema esta siendo actualizado. Perfecta: El contrato ha sido firmado. Modal: Este problema deberia ser escalado. Infinitivo pasivo: El informe necesita ser enviado para el viernes.

grammar.tip_text: La pasiva suena bien en: reportes, noticias, ciencia, entornos formales. Evitala cuando: el agente es importante (di quien lo hizo), en comunicacion directa y conversacional, y cuando suena ambiguo. Regla: si la oracion activa es clara y directa, quizas no necesitas la pasiva.
table: The form needs to be signed.

dialogue.scene: Usando la voz pasiva en un reporte profesional
lines: A
Has the audit been completed?
Se ha completado la auditoria?
B
It is still being reviewed by the compliance team. It should be finalized by Thursday.
Todavia esta siendo revisada por el equipo de cumplimiento. Deberia estar finalizada para el jueves.
A
When was the last internal review conducted?
Cuando se realizo la ultima revision interna?
B
It was carried out in March. The findings have been compiled and a report is being drafted as we speak.
Se realizo en marzo. Los hallazgos han sido compilados y se esta redactando un informe en este momento.
A
Does the proposal need to be revised before submission?
La propuesta necesita ser revisada antes de la presentacion?
B
Yes, two sections need to be rewritten. Once that is done, it will be sent for final approval.
Si, dos secciones necesitan ser reescritas. Una vez hecho eso, sera enviada para aprobacion final.

speaking.scene: Usando la voz pasiva en un reporte profesional
speaking.prompt: "Give a project status update using passive voice."
speaking.response_hint: "It has been... / It is being... / It should be... / It needs to be... / It was completed..."

units.title: Presentaciones Profesionales
units.description: Estructura, lenguaje y tecnicas para dar presentaciones claras y convincentes en ingles.

reading.text_en:
A great presentation in English follows a clear structure and uses signposting language to guide your audience:

Opening: Good morning everyone. Today I am going to walk you through our Q3 results.
Signposting: Let us start with... / Moving on to... / As you can see from the chart... / To summarize...
Handling questions: That is a great question. / Could you clarify what you mean by...?
Closing: To wrap up, I want to leave you with three key takeaways.
reading.text_es: Una gran presentacion en ingles sigue una estructura clara y usa lenguaje de senal. Apertura: Buenos dias. Hoy les voy a presentar los resultados del Q3. Senales: Empecemos con... / Pasando a... / Como pueden ver en el grafico... / Para resumir... Cierre: Para cerrar, quiero dejarles tres puntos clave.
vocab: Let us start with / First of all...
I would be happy to take questions.
extra_vocab: I am happy to take questions

grammar.tip_text: Si no sabes la respuesta: That is a great question — I will need to follow up on that. Si necesitas tiempo: Could you clarify what you mean by...? Si la pregunta esta fuera del tema: That is slightly outside the scope of today, but I am happy to discuss it offline.
table: Today I will walk you through...
Good morning, I will walk you through...
As you can see from this chart...
As you can see, revenue has increased.
Moving on to the next point...
Turning now to our Q4 forecast...
To wrap up, the three key takeaways are...

lines: P
Good morning everyone. Today I will be presenting our market expansion strategy for 2025.
Buenos dias a todos. Hoy presentare nuestra estrategia de expansion de mercado para 2025.
P
Let us start with the current landscape. As you can see from this slide, our market share has grown 12% YoY.
Empecemos con el panorama actual. Como pueden ver en esta diapositiva, nuestra participacion de mercado ha crecido 12% interanual.
P
Moving on to our proposed strategy — we are targeting three new markets: Colombia, Chile, and Peru.
Pasando a nuestra estrategia propuesta — estamos apuntando a tres nuevos mercados: Colombia, Chile y Peru.
P
To wrap up, I want to leave you with three key takeaways. Any questions?
Para cerrar, quiero dejarles tres puntos clave. Alguna pregunta?
Q
What is the expected ROI on the expansion?
Cual es el ROI esperado de la expansion?
P
Great question. Based on our projections, we anticipate an 18-month payback period with a 35% ROI.
Excelente pregunta. Basado en nuestras proyecciones, anticipamos un periodo de recuperacion de 18 meses con un ROI del 35%.

speaking.prompt: "Give a 2-minute presentation on a topic you know well."
speaking.response_hint: "Today I will walk you through... / Let us start with... / As you can see... / Moving on to... / To wrap up..."
phrases: Today I will walk you through...
As you can see from this...
To summarize, the key points are...
I am happy to take questions.

chips: Today I will walk you through...
As you can see from this...
To summarize, the key points are...
I am happy to take questions.

units.title: Explicar Datos y Tendencias
units.description: El lenguaje para describir graficos, numeros y tendencias. Esencial para analisis y reportes.

reading.text_en:
Data-driven communication requires precise vocabulary:

Trends: Revenue has increased significantly / Sales have leveled off / Costs have declined sharply.
Comparisons: Q3 results are considerably higher than Q2 / This figure is three times higher than last year.
Causes: This spike can be attributed to... / The decline was driven by...
Predictions: We project a 15% growth / It is forecast to stabilize by Q4.
reading.text_es: La comunicacion basada en datos requiere vocabulario preciso. Tendencias: Los ingresos han aumentado significativamente. Comparaciones: Los resultados del Q3 son considerablemente mas altos que el Q2. Causas: Este pico puede atribuirse a... Predicciones: Proyectamos un crecimiento del 15%.
vocab: en comparacion con / en relacion a

grammar.tip_text: SUBIR: rise, increase, grow, climb, surge, jump, spike. BAJAR: fall, decrease, decline, drop, plummet, dip. ESTABILIZAR: level off, plateau, stabilize, remain stable. Modifica con adverbios: RAPIDLY (rapidamente), STEADILY (constantemente), SLIGHTLY (levemente), SIGNIFICANTLY (significativamente).
table: driven by, attributed to, resulting from

lines: A
Can you walk us through the Q3 performance figures?
Puedes guiarnos por las cifras de desempeno del Q3?
B
Of course. Revenue increased by 14% compared to the same period last year. This growth can be attributed primarily to our new enterprise accounts.
Por supuesto. Los ingresos aumentaron un 14% en comparacion con el mismo periodo del ano pasado. Este crecimiento puede atribuirse principalmente a nuestras nuevas cuentas empresariales.
A
And what about costs?
Y que hay de los costos?
B
Operating costs have leveled off after rising sharply in Q1. We are projecting a 3% reduction by year-end.
Los costos operativos se han estabilizado despues de aumentar drasticamente en el Q1. Estamos proyectando una reduccion del 3% para fin de ano.
A
What does that mean for our EBITDA?
Que significa eso para nuestro EBITDA?
B
Our EBITDA margin is forecast to reach 22%, which is considerably higher than the industry average of 17%.
Se pronostica que nuestro margen EBITDA alcanzara el 22%, que es considerablemente mayor que el promedio de la industria del 17%.

speaking.prompt: "Describe a trend or set of data from your work or studies."
speaking.response_hint: "Revenue rose sharply... / Costs have leveled off... / This can be attributed to... / We project..."
phrases: The figure is significantly higher/lower than...

units.title: Argumentar Ideas con Estructura
units.description: De la opinion a la persuasion. Argumenta con datos, anticipacion y conclusion en ingles profesional.

reading.text_en:
Effective argumentation in English follows a clear architecture:

Position: I firmly believe that remote work delivers higher productivity.
Evidence: A Stanford study found a 13% increase in output among remote workers.
Counterargument: While some argue that collaboration suffers, modern tools largely compensate.
Reinforcement: Furthermore, employee retention rates improve significantly under hybrid models.
Conclusion: Therefore, I recommend transitioning to a full hybrid framework.
reading.text_es: La argumentacion efectiva sigue una arquitectura clara. Posicion: Creo firmemente que el trabajo remoto entrega mayor productividad. Evidencia: Un estudio de Stanford encontro un aumento del 13%. Contraargumento: Aunque algunos argumentan que la colaboracion sufre... Conclusion: Por tanto, recomiendo...
vocab: I firmly believe / I strongly argue that
While it is true that... / Granted that...

grammar.tip_text: Position: 'I firmly believe...' Evidence: 'Research shows / The data indicates...' Analysis: 'This means that...' Link: 'Therefore, I recommend...' Usala en cualquier presentacion, debate o email profesional. Es la diferencia entre opinar y argumentar.
table: I firmly believe / I strongly argue
I firmly believe this is the best approach.
While it is true that... / Granted...

lines: A
Should we move to a subscription model?
Deberiamos movernos a un modelo de suscripcion?
B
I firmly believe we should. Evidence from our top three competitors shows subscription models generate 40% higher LTV.
Creo firmemente que si. La evidencia de nuestros tres principales competidores muestra que los modelos de suscripcion generan un 40% mayor LTV.
A
But what about the transition risk?
Pero que hay del riesgo de transicion?
B
Granted, there is short-term churn risk. However, research indicates that customers who convert stay 3x longer on average.
Concedido, hay riesgo de abandono a corto plazo. Sin embargo, la investigacion indica que los clientes que convierten permanecen 3 veces mas tiempo en promedio.
A
What would you recommend as a first step?
Que recomendarias como primer paso?
B
I recommend a 90-day pilot with our SMB segment. The data will clearly demonstrate whether the model translates to our market.
Recomiendo un piloto de 90 dias con nuestro segmento SMB. Los datos demostraran claramente si el modelo se traduce a nuestro mercado.

speaking.prompt: "Should we [major business decision]? Argue your position."
speaking.response_hint: "I firmly believe... / Evidence shows... / While it is true that... / Furthermore... / Therefore..."

units.title: Negociacion Basica en Ingles
units.description: El lenguaje de la negociacion: proponer, contraoferta, conceder y cerrar acuerdos profesionalmente.

reading.text_en:
Negotiation in English requires precise, tactful language:

Opening position: We were thinking along the lines of... / Our initial offer would be...
Counterproposal: That is slightly above our budget. Could we explore a figure closer to...?
Concession: If you could commit to a 12-month contract, we would be able to offer a 10% discount.
Agreement: I think we can work with that. Let us put that in writing.
Delay: I would need to take this back to my team before committing.
reading.text_es: La negociacion requiere lenguaje preciso y tactico. Posicion de apertura: Estabamos pensando en algo como... Contrapropuesta: Eso esta un poco por encima de nuestro presupuesto. Podriamos explorar una cifra mas cercana a...? Concesion: Si pudieran comprometerse a un contrato de 12 meses, podriamos ofrecer un 10% de descuento.
vocab: We were thinking along the lines of...
Could we explore / Would you consider...
If you could... we would be able to...
I would need to take this back to...
I think we can work with that.
extra_vocab: take this back to my team

grammar.tip_text: En negociaciones en ingles, el silencio despues de una propuesta NO es incomodidad — es una herramienta. Despues de hacer una oferta, di tu numero y espera. Quien habla primero generalmente cede mas. Tambien funciona: I need to think about this. Let us revisit this tomorrow.
table: We were thinking along the lines of...
That is slightly above our range.
That works, but could we adjust?
If you commit to X, we can offer Y.
If you sign now, we offer 15% off.
I think we can work with that.
Let us put that in writing.

lines: A
We are very interested in the partnership. What are you proposing?
Estamos muy interesados en la asociacion. Que estan proponiendo?
B
We were thinking along the lines of a revenue-share model — 70/30 in your favor for the first year.
Estabamos pensando en algo como un modelo de reparto de ingresos: 70/30 a su favor el primer ano.
A
That is slightly more conservative than what we had in mind. Would you consider 80/20?
Eso es un poco mas conservador de lo que teniamos en mente. Considerarian 80/20?
B
That is a stretch for us. If you could commit to a 24-month agreement, we could offer 75/25 and include dedicated account support.
Eso nos estira mucho. Si pudieran comprometerse a un acuerdo de 24 meses, podriamos ofrecer 75/25 e incluir soporte de cuenta dedicado.
A
I would need to take that back to my team before committing. Can we reconvene Thursday?
Necesitaria consultarlo con mi equipo antes de comprometerme. Podemos reunirnos de nuevo el jueves?
B
Of course. I think we are close to something workable.
Por supuesto. Creo que estamos cerca de algo factible.

speaking.prompt: "Simulate a business negotiation. Start with an offer."
speaking.response_hint: "We were thinking along the lines of... / Would you consider...? / If you could... we would... / I think we can work with that."

That is slightly above our budget.
If you could commit to..., we would offer...
I need to take this back to my team.
I think we can work with that.

units.title: Resolucion de Problemas Profesional
units.description: Identifica, analiza y propone soluciones a problemas en ingles con estructura y confianza.

reading.text_en:
Problem-solving in English requires structured communication:

Identifying the problem: We are experiencing a bottleneck in the approval process / There seems to be a gap in communication.
Analysis: The root cause appears to be... / It seems to stem from...
Proposing solutions: One possible approach would be... / Have we considered...?
Implementation: If we were to implement this, the first step would be... / Going forward, I suggest we...
reading.text_es: La resolucion de problemas requiere comunicacion estructurada. Identificar: Estamos experimentando un cuello de botella... Analisis: La causa raiz parece ser... Proponer: Un posible enfoque seria... Implementar: Si implementaramos esto, el primer paso seria...
vocab: there seems to be / we are experiencing
the root cause appears to be
extra_vocab: en adelante / de aqui en adelante

grammar.tip_text: En ingles profesional, las mejores propuestas suenan como preguntas o posibilidades, no ordenes. En vez de: Do this. Di: One possible approach would be... / Have we considered...? / What if we tried...? / I would suggest exploring... Esto genera buy-in del equipo.
table: There is a gap in our process.
The root cause appears to be...
It seems to stem from outdated systems.

lines: A
We have a serious issue — client response times are consistently above 48 hours.
Tenemos un problema serio: los tiempos de respuesta al cliente estan consistentemente por encima de las 48 horas.
B
I have noticed that too. The root cause appears to be the current approval chain — every response requires sign-off from three people.
Lo he notado tambien. La causa raiz parece ser la cadena de aprobacion actual: cada respuesta requiere visto bueno de tres personas.
A
So how do we fix it?
Entonces como lo arreglamos?
B
One possible approach would be to empower team leads to approve standard responses directly. This would allow us to reduce response time by at least 60%.
Un posible enfoque seria empoderar a los lideres del equipo para aprobar respuestas estandar directamente. Esto nos permitiria reducir el tiempo de respuesta en al menos un 60%.
A
Have we considered a template library?
Hemos considerado una biblioteca de plantillas?
B
Great point. Going forward, I suggest we combine both: a template library with direct approval authority for tier-1 issues.
Excelente punto. En adelante, sugiero que combinemos ambas: una biblioteca de plantillas con autoridad de aprobacion directa para problemas de nivel 1.

speaking.prompt: "Describe a problem and propose a solution."
speaking.response_hint: "We are experiencing... / The root cause appears to be... / One possible approach would be... / This would allow us to... / Going forward..."
phrases: We are experiencing a problem with...
The root cause appears to be...

chips: We are experiencing a problem with...
The root cause appears to be...

units.title: Phrasal Verbs Avanzados
units.description: Los phrasal verbs que usan los profesionales nativos. Sofisticados pero naturales.

reading.text_en:
Advanced phrasal verbs appear in professional conversations, business writing, and high-level discussions:

We need to iron out a few details. (resolve minor issues)
The meeting was called off at the last minute. (cancelled)
We should build on this success. (use as foundation)
The proposal was turned down by the board. (rejected)
We need to follow through on our commitments. (complete what was promised)
reading.text_es: Los phrasal verbs avanzados aparecen en conversaciones profesionales. Necesitamos resolver algunos detalles (iron out). La reunion fue cancelada a ultimo momento (called off). Deberiamos aprovechar este exito (build on). La propuesta fue rechazada por la junta (turned down). Necesitamos cumplir nuestros compromisos (follow through).

grammar.tip_text: SEPARABLE con pronombre en medio: turn IT down (correcto). turn down IT (incorrecto). INSEPARABLE: build on success — no se puede separar con pronombre. Para recordar: si el objeto es un pronombre (it, them, him), va entre el verbo y la particula en los separables.
table: We need to iron out the contract terms.
The summit was called off due to weather.
Let us build on what worked last time.
We must follow through on this.
Please flag up any concerns immediately.

dialogue.scene: Usando phrasal verbs en conversacion profesional
lines: A
Did the board approve the acquisition proposal?
La junta aprobo la propuesta de adquisicion?
B
They turned it down, but they asked us to iron out a few concerns and resubmit next quarter.
La rechazaron, pero nos pidieron que resolviéramos algunas preocupaciones y la enviáramos de nuevo el proximo trimestre.
A
What were the main issues they flagged up?
Cuales fueron los principales problemas que senalaron?
B
Primarily the integration timeline. They felt we had not followed through on lessons from the last merger.
Principalmente el cronograma de integracion. Sintieron que no habiamos concretado las lecciones del ultimo fusionamiento.
A
Fair point. We should build on the feedback and come back with a stronger case.
Punto justo. Deberiamos aprovechar la retroalimentacion y volver con un argumento mas solido.
B
Agreed. I will also check whether the Q2 review meeting was called off — I want to use that slot for a pre-presentation run-through.
De acuerdo. Tambien verificare si la reunion de revision del Q2 fue cancelada — quiero usar ese espacio para un ensayo de pre-presentacion.

speaking.scene: Usando phrasal verbs en conversacion profesional
speaking.prompt: "Tell me about a recent work situation using advanced phrasal verbs."
speaking.response_hint: "We need to iron out... / The meeting was called off. / We should build on... / They turned it down. / We must follow through on..."

units.title: Collocations Profesionales
units.description: Las combinaciones de palabras que suenan naturales en entornos de negocios. El sello del ingles avanzado.

reading.text_en:
Professional collocations are the word combinations that native speakers use instinctively in business contexts. Getting them right is a major differentiator at B2 level:

reach a consensus / meet a deadline / conduct a review / address a concern / drive growth / gain momentum / achieve targets / sustain engagement

The wrong collocations are grammatically correct but sound unnatural: do consensus / make a deadline / perform a review.
reading.text_es: Las colocaciones profesionales son las combinaciones de palabras que los nativos usan instintivamente en negocios. Equivocarse gramaticalmente puede sonar correcto pero suena poco natural: reach a consensus, meet a deadline, conduct a review, address a concern, drive growth, gain momentum.

grammar.tip_text: MAKE: make a profit, make a decision, make progress, make a case. TAKE: take action, take responsibility, take the lead, take a risk. ACHIEVE: achieve targets, achieve consensus, achieve results. DRIVE: drive growth, drive change, drive engagement. Aprendelas por grupos tematicos.
table: We need to reach a consensus quickly.
The team met every deadline this quarter.
We need to sustain our momentum.
I want to address your main concerns.

dialogue.scene: Usando collocaciones en conversacion de negocios
lines: A
Have we reached a consensus on the new pricing model?
Hemos llegado a un consenso sobre el nuevo modelo de precios?
B
Not yet. We conducted a review last week, but a few stakeholders still want to address their concerns before committing.
Todavia no. Realizamos una revision la semana pasada, pero algunos stakeholders aun quieren abordar sus preocupaciones antes de comprometerse.
A
We need to meet the Q3 deadline. Can we gain momentum this week?
Necesitamos cumplir el plazo del Q3. Podemos ganar impulso esta semana?
B
Yes. I have scheduled two alignment meetings. If we drive the right conversations, we should achieve consensus by Friday.
Si. He programado dos reuniones de alineacion. Si impulsamos las conversaciones correctas, deberíamos lograr consenso para el viernes.
A
Good. Let us make sure we sustain that momentum into Q4.
Bien. Asegurémonos de sostener ese impulso hasta el Q4.

speaking.scene: Usando collocaciones en conversacion de negocios
speaking.prompt: "Describe your current work or project using professional collocations."
speaking.response_hint: "We reached a consensus on... / We need to meet the deadline for... / I will conduct a review of... / This drives growth by..."
phrases: We need to meet the deadline for...
I will conduct a review of...

chips: We need to meet the deadline for...
I will conduct a review of...

units.title: Idioms Comunes en Ingles Profesional
units.description: Los modismos que escucharas en reuniones, presentaciones y conversaciones de negocios reales.

reading.text_en:
Business idioms appear constantly in professional English. Understanding them is the difference between following a conversation and truly participating:

Let us cut to the chase. (get to the point)
We are on the same page. (agree/aligned)
This is a game changer. (transformative development)
Let us think outside the box. (think creatively)
We need to move the needle. (make measurable progress)
The ball is in their court. (it is their decision now)
reading.text_es: Los idioms de negocios aparecen constantemente. Entenderlos es la diferencia entre seguir una conversacion y realmente participar: cut to the chase (ir al grano), on the same page (alineados), game changer (algo transformador), outside the box (creativamente), move the needle (progreso medible), the ball is in their court (es su decision).
vocab: ir al grano / directo al punto
the ball is in their court
extra_vocab: the ball is in their court

grammar.tip_label: Como usar idioms sin sonar forzado
grammar.tip_text: Usa MAXIMO 2-3 idioms por conversacion. Usalos en el momento natural — no los fuercas. Si no estas 100% seguro del contexto, parafrasea. Los idioms mas seguros en negocios: on the same page, cut to the chase, move the needle. Los mas informales (evita en presentaciones formales): hit the ground running, back to square one.
table: Let us cut to the chase: we need more budget.
Are we on the same page about this?
This technology is a game changer.
Nothing we tried moved the needle.
We need to think outside the box.
the ball is in their court
We sent the offer. The ball is in their court.

lines: A
Let us cut to the chase. Are we aligned on the budget increase?
Vayamos al grano. Estamos alineados en el aumento de presupuesto?
B
I think we are on the same page on the numbers. The question is whether the board will see this as a game changer.
Creo que estamos alineados en los numeros. La pregunta es si la junta vera esto como algo transformador.
A
We need them to think outside the box here — traditional metrics will not capture the full value.
Necesitamos que piensen creativamente aqui: las metricas tradicionales no capturaran el valor completo.
B
Exactly. And if we frame it correctly, this could really move the needle on our market position.
Exactamente. Y si lo enmarcamos correctamente, esto podria generar un progreso real en nuestra posicion de mercado.
A
We have presented our case. Now the ball is in their court.
Hemos presentado nuestro caso. Ahora es su turno de decidir.

speaking.prompt: "Describe a business situation or decision using at least 3 idioms naturally."
speaking.response_hint: "Let us cut to the chase. / Are we on the same page? / This is a game changer. / We need to move the needle. / Think outside the box."
phrases: Let us cut to the chase.
Are we on the same page?
This is a real game changer.
We need to move the needle.
Let us think outside the box.
The ball is in their court.

chips: Let us cut to the chase.
Are we on the same page?
This is a real game changer.
We need to move the needle.
Let us think outside the box.
The ball is in their court.

units.title: Formal vs Informal: El Registro Correcto
units.description: El mismo mensaje, diferentes tonos. Dominar el registro es la marca del profesional bilingue avanzado.

reading.text_en:
Advanced English users can switch register — the level of formality — depending on context:

Informal: Just wanted to check if you have had a chance to look at the doc.
Formal: I am writing to enquire whether you have had the opportunity to review the document.

Informal: The meeting got pushed back.
Formal: The meeting has been rescheduled.

Matching the register to the relationship and context is a sophisticated skill that marks you as truly fluent.
reading.text_es: Los usuarios avanzados de ingles pueden cambiar el registro segun el contexto. Informal: Solo queria saber si pudiste revisar el doc. Formal: Le escribo para consultar si ha tenido la oportunidad de revisar el documento. Informal: La reunion se recorrio. Formal: La reunion ha sido reprogramada.
vocab: I am writing to vs Hey
please find attached vs here is
formal vs casual al enviar archivo
as per our discussion vs like we said

grammar.tip_text: FORMAL: oraciones completas, vocabulario sofisticado, no contracciones, tratamiento de usted. INFORMAL: contracciones (I am → I'm), palabras coloquiales, frases cortas, humor. SEMIINFORMAL (el mas comun en tech/startups): Hi Carlos, Hope you are well. Direct, warm, professional. Ni muy rigido ni muy casual.
table: Estimado/a — para clientes, ejecutivos, primeros contactos
Many thanks for your prompt response.

dialogue.scene: Adaptando el tono segun el contexto
lines: I
I would like to ask for some advice. I received an email from a client that seemed quite formal. How should I respond?
Quisiera pedir consejo. Recibi un email de un cliente que parecia bastante formal. Como deberia responder?
A
Always match the register of the person you are writing to. If they write formally, respond formally.
Siempre emparejar el registro de la persona a quien escribes. Si escriben formalmente, responde formalmente.
I
What is the difference in practice?
Cual es la diferencia en la practica?
A

For example: informal is Hi Carlos, Thanks for sending this over, let me take a look. Formal would be: Dear Mr. Garcia, Thank you for forwarding the documentation. I will review it and revert at my earliest convenience.

Por ejemplo: informal seria Hola Carlos, gracias por enviar esto, le echo un ojo. Formal seria: Estimado Sr. Garcia, le agradezco el envio de la documentacion. La revisare y me pondre en contacto a la brevedad.
I
How do I know which to use?
Como se cual usar?
A
Consider the relationship, the industry, and the seniority level. When in doubt, start formal and adapt downward based on their cues.
Considera la relacion, la industria y el nivel de seniority. Ante la duda, empieza formal y adapta hacia abajo segun sus senales.

speaking.scene: Adaptando el tono segun el contexto
speaking.prompt: "Write or say the same message in formal and informal English."
speaking.response_hint: "Formal: Dear..., I am writing to... / Informal: Hi..., Just wanted to... / Semi-formal: Hope you are well..."

units.title: Matices de Significado
units.description: Las diferencias sutiles que separan el ingles avanzado del intermedio. Precision en cada palabra.

reading.text_en:
At B2 level, the difference between near-synonyms matters enormously:

big / large / substantial / considerable / significant
say / claim / argue / maintain / assert / contend
problem / issue / challenge / concern / obstacle
end / conclude / finalize / wrap up / close

Choosing the precise word signals expertise. We faced a challenge sounds professional. We had a problem sounds vague. He claimed the results were valid implies doubt. He confirmed the results were valid implies certainty.
reading.text_es: En el nivel B2, la diferencia entre casi-sinonimos importa enormemente. big/large/substantial/considerable/significant. say/claim/argue/maintain/assert. problem/issue/challenge/concern/obstacle. Elegir la palabra precisa senala experticia. We faced a CHALLENGE suena profesional. He CLAIMED implica duda. He CONFIRMED implica certeza.
vocab: claim = afirmar (con duda), confirm = confirmar (con certeza)
mejorar general, mejorar calidad, maximizar eficiencia

grammar.tip_text: En vez de: big → significant / large / substantial. En vez de: problem → challenge / issue / concern. En vez de: good → effective / robust / compelling / successful. En vez de: bad → concerning / suboptimal / below expectations. En vez de: say → argue / maintain / contend / suggest / indicate. Estas sustituciones elevan instantaneamente tu nivel percibido.
table: He claimed / She confirmed it worked.
I would argue the data supports this.

lines: A
How would you describe the gap in our Q2 results?
Como describirias la brecha en nuestros resultados del Q2?
B
I would call it a significant deviation from our projections, rather than a problem. We faced a specific challenge: delayed product launches.
Lo llamaria una desviacion significativa de nuestras proyecciones, en vez de un problema. Enfrentamos un desafio especifico: lanzamientos de productos retrasados.
A
The CEO is claiming that the team underperformed. Do you agree?
El CEO afirma que el equipo tuvo un rendimiento bajo. Estas de acuerdo?
B
I would argue differently. The data maintains that external factors — supply chain and market volatility — were the primary drivers.
Argumentaria diferente. Los datos sostienen que los factores externos: cadena de suministro y volatilidad del mercado, fueron los principales impulsores.
A
How do we conclude this review?
Como concluimos esta revision?
B
I suggest we finalize the report with three actionable recommendations and close with a clear timeline.
Sugiero que ultimemos el informe con tres recomendaciones accionables y cerremos con un cronograma claro.

speaking.prompt: "Describe a work situation using precise vocabulary — no basic words allowed."
speaking.response_hint: "This is a significant challenge / I would argue that... / The primary concern is... / We need to finalize... / This substantially enhances..."

units.title: Conversaciones Largas y Sostenidas
units.description: Mantener conversaciones de 20+ minutos con fluidez, sin perder el hilo ni el interes del interlocutor.

reading.text_en:
Sustaining a long conversation in English at B2 level requires more than vocabulary — it requires strategic conversation management:

Topic development: Building on what you said about X, I think...
Engagement signals: That is a really interesting perspective. / I had not considered that angle.
Recovery: Let me rephrase that. / What I am getting at is...
Topic transitions: That reminds me of something related... / On a slightly different note...
Depth signals: This gets into the question of... / At a deeper level, what this means is...
reading.text_es: Mantener una conversacion larga requiere mas que vocabulario: gestion estrategica de la conversacion. Desarrollo de tema, senales de compromiso, recuperacion, transiciones, profundidad. Building on what you said... / That is an interesting perspective. / Let me rephrase that. / On a slightly different note...
vocab: that is a really interesting perspective
let me rephrase / what I am getting at is
this gets into the question of...
extra_vocab: a lo que voy / lo que quiero decir
esto lleva a / esto nos adentra en

grammar.tip_text: ESCUCHA ACTIVA: Asiente, parafrasea, pregunta follow-up. PROFUNDIDAD: Nunca respondas solo con SI/NO. Agrega un ejemplo, una opinion, una anecdota. TRANSICIONES: Usa connecting phrases para mover el tema organicamente. RECUPERACION: Si pierdes el hilo, "di": Where were we? / Let me pick up from where I left off. Fluencia sobre perfeccion siempre.
table: Building on your analysis, I think...
I had not considered that perspective.
What I am getting at is...
Let me rephrase — what I mean is...
On a related point, have you noticed...?

dialogue.scene: Conversacion sostenida sobre un tema complejo
lines: A
You mentioned that AI is transforming your industry. How so specifically?
Mencionaste que la IA esta transformando tu industria. En que sentido especificamente?
B
Building on that point — the transformation is less about replacing roles and more about augmenting decision-making. Our analysts now handle three times the workload they used to.

Desarrollando ese punto: la transformacion tiene menos que ver con reemplazar roles y mas con potenciar la toma de decisiones. Nuestros analistas ahora manejan tres veces la carga de trabajo que antes.
A
That is a really interesting perspective. I had assumed the impact would be more disruptive.
Esa es una perspectiva realmente interesante. Habia asumido que el impacto seria mas disruptivo.
B
That is a common assumption. But — and this gets into the question of organizational readiness — the companies seeing disruption are those that have not invested in AI literacy.
Es una suposicion comun. Pero esto lleva a la pregunta de la preparacion organizacional: las empresas que ven disruption son las que no han invertido en alfabetizacion en IA.
A
On a slightly different note, how does that affect hiring?
En un tema ligeramente diferente, como afecta eso a la contratacion?
B
To bring it back to AI — we now prioritize candidates who demonstrate AI fluency over pure technical skills. The landscape has completely shifted.
Para retomar el tema de IA: ahora priorizamos candidatos que demuestran fluidez en IA sobre habilidades puramente tecnicas. El panorama ha cambiado completamente.

speaking.scene: Conversacion sostenida sobre un tema complejo
speaking.prompt: "Let us have a 10-minute conversation on any professional topic. Sustain it!"
speaking.response_hint: "Building on what you said... / That is a fascinating angle. / This gets into the question of... / To bring it back to... / On a slightly different note..."
phrases: Let me rephrase — what I mean is...
This gets into the question of...

chips: Let me rephrase — what I mean is...
This gets into the question of...

units.title: Debates Estructurados
units.description: Presenta, defiende, concede y refuta con estructura de debate avanzado en ingles.

reading.text_en:
Advanced debate in English has a clear architecture:

Opening position: The evidence overwhelmingly supports the view that...
Developing the argument: Not only does this reduce costs, but it also improves quality.
Anticipating opposition: One might argue that... however, this overlooks...
Conceding strategically: I concede that this approach carries risk. Nevertheless...
Powerful close: On balance, the case for X is compelling — the evidence is clear, the precedent exists, and the risk is manageable.
reading.text_es: El debate avanzado tiene una arquitectura clara. Posicion de apertura: La evidencia apoya abrumadoramente... Desarrollar: No solo reduce costos, sino que tambien mejora la calidad. Anticipar oposicion: Algunos podrian argumentar... sin embargo, esto pasa por alto... Concesion estrategica: Concedo que este enfoque conlleva riesgo. Sin embargo...
vocab: one might argue that... however, this overlooks...
en balance / tomando todo en cuenta
the case for X is compelling
el argumento para X es convincente

grammar.tip_text: La concesion estrategica es la herramienta mas poderosa del debatiente avanzado. Al admitir debilidades controladas de tu argumento, ganas credibilidad. Formula: I concede that [weakness]. Nevertheless/However, [stronger counter]. Esto demuestra pensamiento critico y madurez intelectual.
table: Not only is it cheaper, but it is faster.
One might argue... however, this overlooks...
One might claim... but this ignores the fact that...
I concede the risk. Nevertheless, the benefits outweigh it.

dialogue.scene: Debate estructurado sobre un tema complejo
lines: A
Should companies be legally required to report their carbon emissions?
Las empresas deberian estar legalmente obligadas a reportar sus emisiones de carbono?
B
The evidence overwhelmingly supports mandatory disclosure. Not only does transparency drive accountability, but it also enables investors to make informed decisions.
La evidencia apoya abrumadoramente la divulgacion obligatoria. No solo la transparencia impulsa la responsabilidad, sino que tambien permite a los inversores tomar decisiones informadas.
A
But that places a disproportionate burden on smaller businesses.
Pero eso coloca una carga desproporcionada en las empresas mas pequenas.
B
One might argue that. However, this overlooks the tiered framework most proposals include — exemptions exist for SMEs below a certain threshold.
Algunos podrian argumentar eso. Sin embargo, esto pasa por alto el marco escalonado que la mayoria de las propuestas incluyen: existen exenciones para las PYMES por debajo de cierto umbral.
A
I concede that. Nevertheless, compliance costs remain a concern.
Lo concedo. Sin embargo, los costos de cumplimiento siguen siendo una preocupacion.
B
On balance, the case for mandatory reporting is compelling. The long-term systemic benefits — investor confidence, regulatory clarity, competitive fairness — outweigh the short-term friction.

En balance, el argumento para el reporte obligatorio es convincente. Los beneficios sistemicos a largo plazo: confianza de inversores, claridad regulatoria, equidad competitiva, superan la friccion a corto plazo.

speaking.prompt: "Take a position on a controversial topic and defend it through structured debate."
speaking.response_hint: "The evidence overwhelmingly supports... / Not only... but also... / One might argue... however, this overlooks... / I concede... nevertheless... / On balance..."
phrases: One might argue that... however, this overlooks...
On balance, the case is compelling.
The argument for X outweighs the case against.

units.title: Explicar Temas Tecnicos Simples
units.description: Comunicar conceptos tecnicos o especializados a audiencias no tecnicas. La habilidad del experto completo.

reading.text_en:
The ability to explain technical topics to non-technical audiences is one of the most valued skills in professional English:

Simplify without dumbing down: Essentially, blockchain is a shared ledger that cannot be altered retroactively.
Analogies: Think of it like a Google Doc that millions of people share simultaneously — but no one can delete past entries.
Layer complexity: At a basic level... / Going deeper... / For those with a technical background...
Check comprehension: Does that give you a clear picture? / Shall I go into more detail?
reading.text_es: Explicar temas tecnicos a audiencias no tecnicas es una de las habilidades mas valoradas. Simplificar sin trivializar: Esencialmente, blockchain es un libro mayor compartido que no puede ser alterado retroactivamente. Analogias: Piensalo como un Google Doc que millones de personas comparten. Capas de complejidad: En un nivel basico... / Profundizando...
vocab: think of it like / imagine it as...
at a basic level / going deeper...
does that give you a clear picture?
in technical terms / in layperson's terms
the key takeaway for a non-technical audience is...
extra_vocab: does that give you a clear picture

grammar.tip_text: Explica en 3 capas: CAPA 1 (nivel junior): analogia simple, sin jargon. CAPA 2 (nivel medio): estructura basica, terminologia clave. CAPA 3 (nivel experto): detalles tecnicos, matices, limitaciones. Pregunta primero: What is your background on this? — asi sabes desde que capa empezar.
table: At its core, AI is pattern recognition.
Think of it like a very fast calculator.
Going deeper, the algorithm learns iteratively.
Does that give you a clear picture?
Shall I go into more detail?

dialogue.scene: Explicando un concepto tecnico a alguien sin contexto
lines: A
Can you explain what machine learning actually does — in plain terms?
Puedes explicar que hace realmente el machine learning, en terminos sencillos?
B

At its core, machine learning is about teaching computers to learn from examples rather than explicit rules. Think of it like this: instead of programming a computer to detect spam, you show it a million emails labeled spam or not spam — and it figures out the patterns.

En su nucleo, el machine learning consiste en ensenar a las computadoras a aprender de ejemplos en vez de reglas explicitas. Piensalo asi: en vez de programar una computadora para detectar spam, le muestras un millon de emails etiquetados como spam o no spam, y ella descubre los patrones.
A
And how does that differ from traditional programming?
Y en que se diferencia de la programacion tradicional?
B

Going deeper — in traditional programming, humans write every rule. In machine learning, the algorithm writes the rules itself based on data. The key takeaway for a non-technical audience: it gets smarter the more data it sees. Does that give you a clear picture?

Profundizando: en la programacion tradicional, los humanos escriben cada regla. En machine learning, el algoritmo escribe las reglas el mismo basandose en datos. El punto clave para una audiencia no tecnica: se vuelve mas inteligente cuantos mas datos ve. Te da una imagen clara?
A
Yes, very clear. Can you give a real-world business example?
Si, muy claro. Puedes dar un ejemplo de negocio del mundo real?
B
Of course. When Netflix recommends a show, that is machine learning — it has learned your preferences from thousands of data points and predicts what you will enjoy next.
Por supuesto. Cuando Netflix recomienda una serie, eso es machine learning: ha aprendido tus preferencias a partir de miles de puntos de datos y predice lo que disfrutaras a continuacion.

speaking.scene: Explicando un concepto tecnico a alguien sin contexto
speaking.prompt: "Explain a technical concept from your field to someone who has no background."
speaking.response_hint: "At its core... / Think of it like... / Going deeper... / In layperson's terms... / Does that give you a clear picture?"
phrases: Going deeper, the technical reality is...
Does that give you a clear picture?

chips: Going deeper, the technical reality is...
Does that give you a clear picture?

units.title: Expresar Opiniones Profundas
units.description: Del opinion basica a la opinion fundamentada con matiz, evidencia y perspectiva critica.

reading.text_en:
At B2 level, opinions are not just preferences — they are argued positions with nuance, evidence, and intellectual honesty:

I tend to think that... although I recognize the counterarguments.
My view is nuanced — on one hand..., but on the other hand...
The evidence points toward..., though I would caveat that...
It is a complex issue, but if I had to take a position, I would argue that...
I have changed my view on this — I used to think..., but now I believe...
reading.text_es: En B2, las opiniones son posiciones argumentadas con matiz, evidencia y honestidad intelectual. Tiendo a pensar que... aunque reconozco los contraargumentos. Mi perspectiva es matizada: por un lado..., pero por el otro... La evidencia apunta hacia..., aunque advertiria que...
vocab: I have changed my view on this
if I had to take a position, I would argue...
extra_vocab: if I had to take a position
si tuviera que tomar una posicion

grammar.tip_text: Los pensadores mas sofisticados no tienen respuestas absolutas — tienen posiciones informadas y abiertas a revision. Frases que demuestran esto: I tend to think... (no absolutista). I would caveat that... (auto-critico). I have changed my view... (evolucion intelectual). That said... (equilibrio). These are intellectually honest phrases that native advanced speakers use constantly.
table: I tend to think regulation will increase.
My view is nuanced — on one hand...
On one hand efficiency, on the other, quality.
I would caveat that the data has limitations.
I have changed my view on this.
I used to think X, but now I believe Y.

dialogue.scene: Expresando una opinion compleja y matizada
lines: A
What is your view on the future of work? Will offices become obsolete?
Cual es tu opinion sobre el futuro del trabajo? Las oficinas se volvaran obsoletas?
B
My view is nuanced. On one hand, the evidence clearly points toward a permanent shift to hybrid work — productivity data, retention data, and talent pool data all support this.
Mi perspectiva es matizada. Por un lado, la evidencia apunta claramente hacia un cambio permanente hacia el trabajo hibrido: datos de productividad, retencion y talento lo respaldan.
A
And on the other hand?
Y por el otro?
B

On the other hand, I would caveat that the social and creative benefits of in-person work are genuinely undervalued in those studies. Collaboration, culture, mentorship — these are harder to quantify.

Por el otro lado, advertiria que los beneficios sociales y creativos del trabajo presencial estan genuinamente subestimados en esos estudios. Colaboracion, cultura, "mentoria": son mas dificiles de cuantificar.
A
Has your view on this changed?
Ha cambiado tu perspectiva sobre esto?
B
It has, actually. I used to think offices would disappear entirely. Now I believe we are moving toward purpose-driven office use — people come in for collaboration, not execution.

Si, en realidad. Antes pensaba que las oficinas desaparecerian por completo. Ahora creo que nos movemos hacia un uso de oficinas orientado a proposito: la gente viene para colaborar, no para ejecutar.

speaking.scene: Expresando una opinion compleja y matizada
speaking.prompt: "Give your opinion on a complex topic. Include nuance, evidence, and intellectual honesty."
speaking.response_hint: "I tend to think... / My view is nuanced — on one hand... / I would caveat that... / If I had to take a position... / I have changed my view on this."
phrases: My view is nuanced — on one hand...
I have changed my view on this.

chips: My view is nuanced — on one hand...
I have changed my view on this.

units.title: Interaccion en Reuniones en Ingles
units.description: De participante a contributor activo. El lenguaje para intervenir, facilitar y liderar reuniones en ingles.

reading.text_en:
Meetings in English require a specific set of skills — not just language, but meeting management strategies:

Starting: Let us get started. The purpose of today's session is to...
Taking the floor: If I could just come in here... / I would like to build on that point...
Checking understanding: Could you elaborate on that? / Just to clarify — are you suggesting...?
Moving forward: Shall we move on? / Let us park that discussion and return to the main agenda.
Action items: To summarize the actions: X will... by Friday. Y is responsible for...
reading.text_es: Las reuniones en ingles requieren habilidades especificas. Iniciar: Empecemos. El objetivo de hoy es... Tomar la palabra: Si me permiten intervenir... / Me gustaria desarrollar ese punto. Verificar comprension: Podria elaborar sobre eso? Avanzar: Seguimos adelante? / Estacionemos esa discusion. Acciones: Para resumir las acciones: X hara... para el viernes.
vocab: let us get started / the purpose today is
if I could just come in here...
just to clarify — are you suggesting...?
let us park that and return to...
estacionar un tema fuera de agenda
extra_vocab: if I could just come in

grammar.tip_text: En reuniones en ingles, interrumpir educadamente es una habilidad clave. Frases: If I could just come in here... / Sorry to interrupt, but I wanted to add... / Building on that point... / Can I just pick up on something you said? Nunca interrumpas abruptamente. El ingles profesional valora la cortesia estrategica sobre la agresividad.
table: Let us get started. The purpose today is...
Good morning. Let us begin — today's goal is...
If I could just come in here...
Sorry to interrupt — if I could add...
Could you say a bit more about that?
Let us park that and return to...
Let us table that and come back to it.

dialogue.scene: Facilitando y participando en una reunion
lines: F
Good morning everyone. Let us get started — we have a tight agenda. The purpose of today's session is to align on the Q4 roadmap.
Buenos dias a todos. Empecemos: tenemos una agenda ajustada. El objetivo de hoy es alinearnos en el roadmap del Q4.
A
Before we dive in — if I could just come in here — I wanted to flag that the engineering team has a dependency that affects our timeline.
Antes de comenzar: si me permiten intervenir, queria señalar que el equipo de ingenieria tiene una dependencia que afecta nuestro cronograma.
F
Thank you for raising that. Could you elaborate on the dependency briefly?
Gracias por mencionarlo. Podrias elaborar brevemente sobre la dependencia?
A
Sure. We are waiting on API access from the third party — without it, we cannot start the integration sprint.
Claro. Estamos esperando acceso a la API de un tercero: sin eso, no podemos iniciar el sprint de integracion.
F
Understood. Let us park that for now and add it as a standing item — it needs its own discussion. Just to clarify — is the Q4 date still achievable pending that?
Entendido. Estacionemos eso por ahora y añadamoslo como punto recurrente: necesita su propia discusion. Solo para clarificar: la fecha del Q4 sigue siendo alcanzable pendiendo de eso?
A
Yes, if the access comes through by end of next week.
Si, si el acceso llega para el final de la proxima semana.
F
Perfect. To summarize the actions: our tech lead will follow up with the API vendor by Thursday. Maria will present the revised timeline by Friday. Any objections?
Perfecto. Para resumir las acciones: nuestro tech lead hara seguimiento con el proveedor de la API para el jueves. Maria presentara el cronograma revisado para el viernes. Alguna objecion?

speaking.scene: Facilitando y participando en una reunion
speaking.prompt: "Simulate a meeting. One person facilitates, one participates."
speaking.response_hint: "Let us get started. / If I could just come in here... / Could you elaborate? / Let us park that. / To summarize the actions..."
phrases: Let us get started. The purpose today is...
If I could just come in here...
Just to clarify — are you suggesting...?
Let us park that and return to it.

chips: Let us get started. The purpose today is...
If I could just come in here...
Just to clarify — are you suggesting...?
